name: Mask Layer

name: VIA
description: App to quickly check VIA percent modification in a landform

name: BCTS Data Extractor
description: An ArcGIS Pro tool that pulls cutblock, silviculture unit (including WTRA/WTP), and road data from the DBP06 database based on user-supplied identifiers such as Licence ID, Block ID, UBI, or Road Name. It packages the selected features—optionally with configurable buffers around blocks and roads—into your choice of file geodatabase, shapefile, and/or styled KMZ outputs for Google Earth. Results can be delivered as a single combined dataset or automatically split into individual per-block, per-licence, or per-road folders to streamline sharing and field use.

name: Xtools KML
description: An ArcGIS Pro toolbox that brings XTools Pro–style KML workflows into your GIS environment, making it easy to share map data in Google Earth and other KML viewers. Export Layer to KML turns a single feature layer into a polished KMZ with custom labels, HTML attribute popups, and folder organization, while Batch Export to KML handles multiple layers at once—either as separate files or one combined KMZ. KML to Feature Class completes the round trip by importing KML and KMZ files back into a geodatabase as point, line, and polygon feature classes with attributes preserved.

name: Letter Genie
description: Letter Genie automates mail-merge-style letter production for BCTS by filling a Word template with data from an Excel spreadsheet—one personalized letter per row. It also inserts assigned staff contact details and signature images from a staff contacts lookup, and can output Word or PDF files to a chosen folder.
comments: No one ever used this tool so. Never tested post transition so not going to do that and maintain it again until someone asks

name: Polygon Neighbors But Better
description:
tool that identifies neighboring polygons in a feature class and calculates the area of overlap between adjacent features. It automates the Polygon Neighbors workflow by joining neighbor results to the input polygons and adding a new layer to the current map, leaving the original layer unchanged. The tool is useful for spatial analysis tasks that require knowing which polygons border one another and how much they overlap.

This is just a better version of the included Polygon neighbors tool in Arc
comments: This tool would need to be updated to work in ArcPro if we wanted to use again

name: read write test
description: The Read/Write Test tool is an ArcGIS Pro script that evaluates the reliability and performance of a folder location by performing intensive file operations. It creates a test subfolder and generates 1,000 large text files, converts each to PostScript format, and repeatedly reads them back, with timed delays between operations to simulate realistic usage patterns. This tool is intended to verify that a network drive or workspace can sustain heavy read/write activity before it is used for production GIS work.
comments: What was the purpose of creating this tool?

name: SIGG tool Inventory
description:
a searchable web catalogue of scripts and GIS tools used across SIGG. Each entry describes what a tool does, where to find it, who owns it, and who it is intended for. The site is hosted on GitHub Pages and is maintained by collaborators through a simple editor—no separate server or database is required.

*****IMPORTANT****** if you are editing the html file from the script location on the F drive you need to pull it down from github first otherwise you will overwrite the json file that has all the changes people have made since this tool is made to change that file directly on github.

name: Silviweather
description: SilviWeather is a web-based weather monitoring dashboard for British Columbia silviculture operations. It pulls real-time and historical weather from the Open-Meteo API for pre-configured stations across Kamloops, Okanagan, and Kootenays business areas, and flags conditions that can affect planting and field work—frost, heat stress, drought, flood risk, and high wind—against user-configurable thresholds. The tool runs as a single static web page (no API keys, build step, or server required) and can be hosted on GitHub Pages for quick regional weather checks and alert logging.
comments: Dan Arcand in TKA asked for this but never followed up if this was what he wanted. So not really sure if this is complete or how accurate this is

name: SOMC Spatial Creator
description:
** This tool is run after the excel is created in the SOMC Access Database

ArcPro Geoprocessing tool that builds a polygon feature class (SOMC) from an Excel spreadsheet listing species of management concern and their attributes (common name, status, BEC zone, etc.). For each row, it pulls spatial boundaries from BC Geographic Warehouse (BCGW) layers—either Biogeoclimatic Ecosystem Classification (BEC) zones or other referenced feature classes—and clips them to a user-defined area of interest. The tool merges those geometries with the species attributes, dissolves duplicate polygons, writes the result to a geodatabase, and compares the new output to any previous version to flag changes in species or schedule lists.
comments: Need a video for this whole process

name: TOC SOMC Access Database
description: An Access Database to manage SOMC in TOC. Used in conjunction with the SOMC Spatial Creator Tool to create SOMC layer to be used in OPR's
comments: Only GIS Lead and Planning Officer allowed to touch this database, if that's not you hands off because you will probably mess it up

name: Overlap Area Tool
description: The Overlap Area tool compares two polygon feature layers in ArcGIS Pro and quantifies their spatial overlap. It reports overlap area and percentage statistics by feature, adds overlap and residual (non-overlapping) polygon layers to the map, and can optionally export a summary to Excel. The tool uses Basic-licence Pairwise geoprocessing and supports hectares, acres, square meters, or square kilometers.

name: Spatial Archiver
description: Spatial Archiver is an ArcGIS geoprocessing tool that preserves historical snapshots of forest block records from FORESTVIEW.SV_BLOCK (DBP06). For blocks in selected Timber Sale Office areas (TKA, TOC, TKO), it compares each block against the archived_blocks feature class in spatial_archive.gdb and creates a new archive record when spatial geometry, identifiers, areas, or status fields have changed—or when the block is not yet in the archive. Unchanged blocks are skipped, and prior archive versions are marked as HISTORICAL while the new record is stamped with archive metadata (date, user, and reason for archiving).

name: THLB Genie
description: THLB Genie is an ArcGIS Pro toolbox that automates Timber Harvesting Land Base (THLB) and Crown Forest Land Base (CFLB) analysis for a user-defined area of interest. It reads constraint layers from an Excel spreadsheet (parks, OGMAs, riparian buffers, and similar), clips and applies them to the AOI, and produces a classified polygon output where each area is marked as available (Y) or excluded (N) for each constraint. The tool also calculates summary fields (THLB_Total, CFLB_Total) and can produce simplified “Lite” versions showing overall harvestable versus non-harvestable land.
